---
title: "R Notebook"
output: html_notebook
---

```{r}
# --- read (you already do this) ---
na_tokens <- c("NA","N/A","NULL","null")
crashes  <- read.csv("Crashes.csv",  na.strings = na_tokens, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
parties  <- read.csv("Parties.csv",  na.strings = na_tokens, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
victims  <- read.csv("Victims.csv",  na.strings = na_tokens, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
merged_theirs <- read.csv("merged_switrs_data.csv", na.strings = na_tokens, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

library(dplyr)

# --- aggregate parties to crash-level ---
parties_agg <- parties %>%
  group_by(CASE_ID) %>%
  summarise(
    party_count_check = n(), 
    any_dui = any(PARTY_SOBRIETY == "B", na.rm = TRUE),        # B = Had Been Drinking, Under Influence
    any_drug = any(PARTY_DRUG_PHYSICAL == "E", na.rm = TRUE),  # E = Under Drug Influence
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# --- aggregate victims to crash-level ---
# Use VICTIM_DEGREE_OF_INJURY: "1" == killed; other numeric codes indicate injury severity.
victims_agg <- victims %>%
  group_by(CASE_ID) %>%
  summarise(
    victim_count_check = n(),
    killed_check = sum(VICTIM_DEGREE_OF_INJURY == "1", na.rm = TRUE),       # count killed victims
    injured_count = sum(VICTIM_DEGREE_OF_INJURY %in% c("2","3","4","5","6","7"), na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# --- join to crashes (keeps crash-level rows) ---
merged_correct <- crashes %>%
  left_join(parties_agg, by = "CASE_ID") %>%
  left_join(victims_agg, by = "CASE_ID")

# --- quick diagnostics ---
cat("rows: crashes =", nrow(crashes), 
    "  merged_correct =", nrow(merged_correct),
    "  merged_theirs =", nrow(merged_theirs), "\n")

cat("unique CASE_ID in merged_theirs:", length(unique(merged_theirs$CASE_ID)), "\n")
cat("unique CASE_ID in merged_correct:", length(unique(merged_correct$CASE_ID)), "\n")

# Compare key sums (should match if theirs aggregated correctly)
cat("sum NUMBER_KILLED — correct:", sum(merged_correct$NUMBER_KILLED, na.rm = TRUE),
    "  theirs:", sum(merged_theirs$NUMBER_KILLED, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")

# Exact equality test (structure + values) — will flag mismatches
# align sort order and compare — this will error if column sets differ; handle carefully
common_cols <- intersect(names(merged_correct), names(merged_theirs))
manual_sorted <- merged_correct %>% arrange(CASE_ID) %>% select(all_of(common_cols))
theirs_sorted <- merged_theirs  %>% arrange(CASE_ID) %>% select(all_of(common_cols))

cat("all.equal(manual_sorted, theirs_sorted) ->\n")
print(all.equal(manual_sorted, theirs_sorted))
# If not equal, find which CASE_IDs differ
anti_manual <- dplyr::anti_join(manual_sorted, theirs_sorted, by = "CASE_ID")
anti_theirs <- dplyr::anti_join(theirs_sorted, manual_sorted, by = "CASE_ID")
cat("rows in manual but not in theirs:", nrow(anti_manual), 
    " rows in theirs but not in manual:", nrow(anti_theirs), "\n")
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)

# Unique CASE_IDs
cat("unique crash ids - crashes:", length(unique(crashes$CASE_ID)),
    " merged_correct:", length(unique(merged_correct$CASE_ID)),
    " merged_theirs:", length(unique(merged_theirs$CASE_ID)), "\n")

# How many rows per CASE_ID in their file
dup_counts <- merged_theirs %>% count(CASE_ID, name = "rows_theirs")
dup_counts %>% summarise(
  min = min(rows_theirs),
  median = median(rows_theirs),
  mean = mean(rows_theirs),
  max = max(rows_theirs)
) %>% print()

# Show the top 10 most-duplicated crashes
dup_counts %>% arrange(desc(rows_theirs)) %>% head(10) %>% print()

# Totals (if present)
sum_correct_killed <- sum(merged_correct$NUMBER_KILLED, na.rm = TRUE)
sum_theirs_killed  <- sum(merged_theirs$NUMBER_KILLED, na.rm = TRUE)

sum_correct_inj <- sum(merged_correct$NUMBER_INJURED, na.rm = TRUE)
sum_theirs_inj  <- sum(merged_theirs$NUMBER_INJURED, na.rm = TRUE)

cat("NUMBER_KILLED - manual:", sum_correct_killed, "theirs:", sum_theirs_killed, "\n")
cat("NUMBER_INJURED - manual:", sum_correct_inj, "theirs:", sum_theirs_inj, "\n")

# pick one of the most-duplicated CASE_IDs
top_case <- dup_counts %>% arrange(desc(rows_theirs)) %>% slice(1) %>% pull(CASE_ID)

# rows in their file for that case
their_rows <- merged_theirs %>% filter(CASE_ID == top_case)
manual_row <- merged_correct %>% filter(CASE_ID == top_case)

print(top_case)
print("THEIRS rows:")
print(their_rows)
print("MANUAL row:")
print(manual_row)
```
```{r}
# Assuming parties_agg and victims_agg were created as before
merged_fixed <- crashes %>%
  left_join(parties_agg,  by = "CASE_ID") %>%
  left_join(victims_agg,  by = "CASE_ID")

# Should equal crash count
stopifnot(nrow(merged_fixed) == nrow(crashes))
cat("merged_fixed rows =", nrow(merged_fixed), "crashes rows =", nrow(crashes), "\n")

# Compare key totals to their file (to show the error)
cat("NUMBER_KILLED - fixed:", sum(merged_fixed$NUMBER_KILLED, na.rm=TRUE),
    " theirs:", sum(merged_theirs$NUMBER_KILLED, na.rm=TRUE), "\n")

write.csv(merged_fixed, "merged_fixed_switrs.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```
